rule: Live bulletins are generated from this file. Older items remain in intel-archive.html so news never disappears without becoming archive structure. Every bulletin must preserve evidence boundaries and avoid unsupported accusations.

bulletins.label: Epstein Watch
bulletins.headline: Maxwell files new Epstein-documents challenge; prosecutors oppose
bulletins.summary: Reuters reported that Ghislaine Maxwell filed a new habeas corpus petition arguing that newly released Epstein materials undermine her conviction. U.S. prosecutors rejected the claims as untimely or legally baseless. This is a live court-process signal, not proof that the underlying claims are correct.
bulletins.why: Keeps the Epstein lane focused on docket movement, legal arguments, prosecutorial responses, and evidence boundaries rather than viral name-list claims.

bulletins.label: Epstein Watch
bulletins.headline: House Oversight testimony lane adds Epstein staff and scheduling context
bulletins.summary: The Wall Street Journal reported on testimony from Jeffrey Epstein's longtime executive assistant Lesley Groff before the House Oversight Committee. The testimony concerned scheduling, logistics, massage appointments, and claimed lack of knowledge. The association boundary remains essential: testimony and document mentions are not automatic findings of criminal conduct.
bulletins.why: The useful signal is operational structure: staff, scheduling, logistics, records, committee questioning, and where testimony conflicts with survivor or FBI-record claims.

bulletins.label: Energy Shock
bulletins.headline: Hormuz shipments rebound after ceasefire, but maritime risk stays live
bulletins.summary: Reuters reported crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz reached their highest level since the Iran-war disruption began, while shipping traffic remains below normal and vessels continue avoiding central routes. Reports also flagged AIS disruption, route changes, and uncertainty over ceasefire durability.
bulletins.why: Hormuz remains a pressure point where war, energy prices, maritime insurance, sanctions, naval power, and global supply chains converge.

bulletins.label: War File
bulletins.headline: Ukraine announces 40-day campaign to pressure Russia through strikes
bulletins.summary: Reuters reported that President Volodymyr Zelenskiy approved a 40-day campaign intended to influence Russia to end the war, following consultations with Ukraine's security service. Related reporting described preemptive strikes against facilities used to support Russia's war effort.
bulletins.why: Shows the war moving deeper into infrastructure pressure, long-range targeting, energy disruption, air-defense redistribution, and escalation-risk management.

bulletins.label: Cyber Watch
bulletins.headline: Iran reports card-banking disruption after cyberattacks on major lenders
bulletins.summary: Reuters reported that Iran's state-owned Informatics Services Corporation said cyberattacks disrupted card-based banking services at three major banks, temporarily affecting ATM, mobile-banking, and point-of-sale services.
bulletins.why: Cyber shocks are no longer abstract: banking rails, payment systems, wartime attribution, and public trust can all become battlefield surfaces.

bulletins.label: Migration Watch
bulletins.headline: Libya-route shipwreck leaves 51 migrants dead or missing
bulletins.summary: AP reported that a migrant boat capsized off Libya's eastern coast, leaving 51 people dead or missing and 10 survivors. AP cited IOM figures that more than 800 migrants were reported dead or missing on the central Mediterranean route from January to mid-May 2026.
bulletins.why: Migration dashboards must track deaths and disappearances separately from arrivals, asylum applications, interceptions, and political rhetoric.

bulletins.label: Famine Watch
bulletins.headline: UN food agencies warn acute hunger may worsen across 13 hotspots
bulletins.summary: AP reported that FAO and WFP warned acute hunger may worsen in 13 hotspots from June to November 2026, with about 266 million people facing high levels of food insecurity. Conflict, economic shocks, climate events, and funding shortfalls remain central drivers.
bulletins.why: Food stress links war, debt, climate, migration, aid cuts, disease, sanctions, state failure, and social unrest into one measurable pressure system.

bulletins.label: Human Cost
bulletins.headline: UNHCR reports first displacement decline in a decade, but crisis remains massive
bulletins.summary: Reuters reported that forced displacement declined in 2025 for the first time in a decade, while the global refugee population remained around 41.6 million, including Palestinian refugees, and returns often occurred under fragile conditions.
bulletins.why: Displacement figures must separate refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced people, returns, and fragile post-conflict movements rather than turning every number into a slogan.

bulletins.label: AI Watch
bulletins.headline: U.S. presses Meta to submit advanced AI models for government review
bulletins.summary: Reuters reported that the U.S. government is pressing Meta to join other major AI developers in voluntarily submitting advanced models for pre-release review of capabilities and vulnerabilities. The move is framed around national-security concerns including cyber and military risk.
bulletins.why: AI governance is becoming a national-security lane: model access, government review, military use, cyber risk, and corporate-state boundaries are converging.
